none: Baltimore, Inner Harbor West urban renewal : environmental impact statement.

The Inner Harbor West Urban Renewal Project in Baltimore, Maryland, aims to revitalize a 68-acre area near the Central Business District. The project seeks to boost economic viability by integrating moderate and middle-income housing with new commercial facilities. It plans to attract significant private investment and create around 1,300 housing units. While the project promises economic growth, it raises concerns about noise pollution and potential negative impacts on the local housing market. The community is divided, with some fearing displacement and others hopeful for improved living conditions.

Department of Housing and Urban Development
United States Department of the Interior

The Inner Harbor West Urban Renewal Project was proposed to revitalize a significant area in Baltimore, aiming to enhance economic viability and attract private investment. However, the project faced public pushback, particularly from community members concerned about potential displacement and the impact on local housing markets. Despite these concerns, the project has garnered support from those who believe it will lead to improved living conditions and economic growth.
United States. Department of Housing and Urban Development

"The Inner Harbor West Urban Renewal Project can still serve as a fruitful resource in minority business opportunities in such areas as demolition, clearing, hauling, construction contracting, etc." — United States Department of Commerce

"The draft statement provides little or no information on open space, park or recreation areas... If no recreation facilities are planned, the section on Alternatives in the final statement should include a discussion of project modification to provide such facilities." — United States Department of the Interior
